channel.title: Niketan Rane
channel.description: Writing on developer productivity, authentication, machine learning, and building tools that make engineers' lives easier.

item.title: Understanding 2>&1 in 3 Minutes
item.description: Breaking down what command > output.log 2>&1 actually means—file descriptors, stdout vs stderr, and useful patterns like splitting streams and /dev/null.

item.title: The Workflow I've Settled On for AI-Assisted Development
item.description: Using Copilot as a sounding board, separating data from presentation, and turning successful workflows into reusable skills—how AI changed the way I think, not just how I write code.

item.title: Returning to Myself
item.description: A visit back home after two years, rediscovering the people who shaped me, the gestures that feel bigger with age, and a sense of belonging I had almost forgotten.

item.title: Shanashma - A Claude Code Plugin Marketplace for Azure DevOps
item.description: Building a Claude Code plugin with persistent configuration to solve the multi-project Azure DevOps workflow problem, packaged as a shareable plugin marketplace.

item.title: Building a Claude Agent for Continuous Codebase Improvement
item.description: Building a Claude sub-agent workflow to continuously analyze and improve codebases with small, incremental changes—using a finder, implementer, and reviewer pattern.

item.title: Teaching Machines to Find the Right Reviewers
item.description: Building a machine learning recommendation system to intelligently assign code reviewers based on historical patterns, expertise, and context—moving beyond static path-based rules.

item.title: Understanding Authentication: OpenID Connect
item.description: Learn how to outsource user authentication to Microsoft using OpenID Connect. A practical guide with code examples showing how to implement federated authentication in a FastAPI application.

item.title: Understanding Authentication: A Simple Dive into OAuth 2.0
item.description: Explore how authentication works—from basic password-based login to modern, secure methods like OpenID Connect—through the lens of a simple library app.
